title: Watsonia longicollis
taxonomy: Watsonia longicollis is classified under the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This family consists of over 660 species of flowering plants found in various parts of the world. Watsonia longicollis is a species of flowering plant native to South Africa, specifically the Western Cape region. It is further classified under the genus Watsonia, which includes about 90 species of plants. The exact classification of Watsonia longicollis can be broken down as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Iridaceae, Genus: Watsonia, Species: W. longicollis.

intro: Watsonia longicollis, commonly known as the Yellow Flag or King's Candeum, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Watsonia longicollis include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, Watsonia longicollis is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
